instruction: What does the policy establish?

instruction: What does the Information Management Policy Framework consist of?
output:
[Chapter: 3.  Policy Framework and Management | Section: 3.3  Policy | Pages: 6-6]
The Information Management Policy Framework consists of the following:
3.3.1  Policy - Organisation level controls.
3.3.2  Standards, Procedures and Frameworks - defined centrally for standardisation.
3.3.3  Standard Operating Procedures - defined at Division / Shared Services level.
3.3.4  Guidelines and Templates - best practices for reference.
3.3.5  The Policy shall be reviewed by the CITSC at least once every 2 years.
3.3.6  Amendments shall be put up to the CMC for consideration.

instruction: Which component of the framework defines organisation level controls?
output:
[Chapter: 3.  Policy Framework and Management | Section: 3.3  Policy | Pages: 6-6]
3.3.1  Policy - Organisation level controls.

instruction: Where are standards, procedures and frameworks defined according to the framework?
output:
[Chapter: 3.  Policy Framework and Management | Section: 3.3  Policy | Pages: 6-6]
3.3.2  Standards, Procedures and Frameworks - defined centrally for standardisation.

instruction: How often shall the Policy be reviewed by the CITSC?
output:
[Chapter: 3.  Policy Framework and Management | Section: 3.3  Policy | Pages: 6-6]
3.3.5  The Policy shall be reviewed by the CITSC at least once every 2 years.

instruction: To which body shall amendments be submitted for consideration?
output:
[Chapter: 3.  Policy Framework and Management | Section: 3.3  Policy | Pages: 6-6]
3.3.6  Amendments shall be put up to the CMC for consideration.

instruction: To which authority must amendments be presented for review?
output:
